name: Strawberry Meringue Cake
recipeInstructions:
1. Genoise layer: Preheat oven to 350°F (175°C). Break eggs into a bowl of an electric mixer and whisk in salt, then sugar. Place the bowl over a pan of simmering water and whisk until just lukewarm. Whip with the mixer until cold and increased in volume, about 4-5 minutes. Mix cake flour and cornstarch, sift over the egg foam in three or four additions, folding in with a rubber spatula. Pour batter into a buttered and paper-lined 10-inch round pan and level. Bake for about 30 minutes until well-risen, golden, and beginning to shrink from the sides. Unmold immediately and cool on a rack.
2. Divide the cooled genoise into three layers with a sharp, serrated knife. Place one layer on a cardboard or platter and moisten with 1/3 of the syrup using a brush. Spread with 1/2 the filling. Repeat with the second layer. Place the last layer on and moisten with the remaining syrup.
3. Lemon kirsch syrup: Combine water and sugar in a small pan, bring to a boil, and cool. Stir in lemon juice and kirsch.
4. Strawberry filling: Reserve six of the best strawberries as a decoration. Rinse, hull, and slice the remaining berries. Place 1/4 of the berries in a saucepan with the sugar and bring to a boil. Combine lemon juice, kirsch, and cornstarch, stirring off the heat. Stir into the strawberry mixture off the heat. Return to a boil, stirring, and cook for 2 minutes. Remove from heat, cool, and stir in the remaining sliced berries.
5. Covering meringue: Preheat oven to 400°F (200°C) and set the rack in the middle level. Combine egg whites and sugar in a bowl of an electric mixer. Whisk over simmering water until the egg whites are hot and sugar is dissolved. Beat on medium speed until cold and risen in volume, but not dry. Spread the cake with meringue. Pipe decoration on top using a pastry bag fitted with a star tube. Press toasted, sliced almonds against the side of the cake. Place the cake on a cookie sheet and bake for about 3-4 minutes. Remove from the oven and cool.
6. Decorate with the reserved strawberries.
